paper_title: Organic Residue Analysis of Experimental Cooking Vessels

methods.method_name: Organic Residue Extraction and Lipid Recovery Measurement
methods.description: Analysis of organic residues from experimental cooking vessels (rims, bases, burnt-on residues) using established analytical procedures to measure lipid recovery. This method prepares samples for subsequent analysis and quantifies the amount of extractable lipid.

steps.description: Collect rims, bases, and 'burnt-on' residues from experimental vessels.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples from experimental cooking vessels for residue analysis

steps.description: Perform organic residue analysis using well-established analytical procedures described in OSM 1.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform organic residue extraction and processing using established protocol

steps.description: Measure lipid recoveries from the vessels and surface residues.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure lipid concentration or recovery from extracts

methods.method_name: Isotopic Analysis of Fatty Acids using GC-C-IRMS
methods.description: Measurement of stable carbon isotope ratios (δ 13 C) of specific fatty acids (C 16:0 and C 18:0) extracted from organic residues, using Gas Chromatography-Combustion-Isotope Ratio Mass Spectrometry (GC-C-IRMS). This technique is used to infer the source of the organic matter (e.g., C3/C4 plants, ruminant/non-ruminant animals).

steps.description: Prepare lipid extracts for GC-C-IRMS analysis, focusing on major fatty acids C 16:0 and C 18:0.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare fatty acid samples for isotopic analysis by GC-C-IRMS

steps.description: Measure the δ 13 C values of the major fatty acids, C 16:0 and C 18:0, using GC-C-IRMS.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure stable carbon isotopes (δ13C) of C16:0 and C18:0 fatty acids using GC-C-IRMS

steps.description: Calculate Δ 13 C values (Δ 13 C = δ 13 C 18:0 - δ 13 C 16:0).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate delta 13C difference between C18:0 and C16:0 fatty acids

steps.description: Interpret δ 13 C and Δ 13 C values to infer the origin of the organic matter (e.g., C3 plants, C4 plants, ruminant fats, non-ruminant fats) by comparing to known isotopic ranges and plotting results (e.g., Fig. 7).
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret isotopic data (δ13C, Δ13C) to determine source material origin based on reference ranges
inputs: known isotopic reference data (C3, C4, ruminant, non-ruminant)
outputs: inferred source material origin (e.g., C4 plant, ruminant fat)
